mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% versus above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the conditional mean return difference is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy aggressively, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns remain positive when both series exceed their 10th percentile thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month expansions in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflect rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term momentum rallies in equity markets.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during identified post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when applied outside this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures indicate robust consumer spending momentum, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings growth and supports positive forward equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for RSAFS remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no correlation with retail sales growth across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive forward equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when both labor indicators move in the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 5.0 percent year-over-year threshold triggers immediate market expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 5.0 threshold, or the conditional return distribution shows no statistical difference from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion phase signal weakening real estate construction activity and reduced consumer leverage capacity, which drags on short-term equity momentum and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when HOUST declines during late-cycle expansion periods, or the signal fails to predict returns when applied in early-cycle or recession regimes.
